description: Living World & Real-Time Conversation Engine — gRPC streaming conversation microservice, full voice pipeline, autonomous NPC behavior, pathfinding, building collision/interiors, language-integrated commerce, and plugin SDKs for JS/Unreal/Unity/Godot

userStories.title: Protobuf schema for conversation service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need protobuf service definitions so that all clients share a single conversation contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Create proto/conversation.proto with service InsimulConversation
Define ConversationStream RPC: bidirectional streaming (stream ConversationRequest <-> stream ConversationResponse)
ConversationRequest supports oneof: TextInput (text, session_id, character_id, language_code), AudioChunk (data bytes, encoding, sample_rate, session_id, character_id), SystemCommand

ConversationResponse supports oneof: TextChunk (text, is_final, language_code, session_id), AudioChunk (data, encoding, sample_rate, duration_ms), FacialData (repeated Viseme with phoneme/weight/duration_ms), ActionTrigger (action_type, target_id, parameters map), ConversationMeta (session_id, state enum)
Define NpcToNpcStream RPC for autonomous NPC-NPC conversations
Install @grpc/proto-loader and @grpc/grpc-js as dependencies
Compile protobuf to TypeScript types using ts-proto or proto-loader
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: LLM provider interface and Gemini streaming implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pluggable LLM provider so the conversation service can use Gemini with streaming and swap providers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/services/conversation/providers/llm-provider.ts with ILLMProvider interface
Interface has streamCompletion(prompt, context, options) returning AsyncIterable<string> for token-by-token delivery
Options include: temperature, maxTokens, languageCode, characterPersonality, conversationHistory
Create server/services/conversation/providers/gemini-provider.ts implementing ILLMProvider
Gemini provider uses generateContentStream for streaming tokens
Create provider registry with registerProvider(name, factory) and getProvider(name)
Configuration via env vars: LLM_PROVIDER, GEMINI_API_KEY
Typecheck passes
Unit test: provider registry resolves correctly, Gemini provider instantiates

userStories.title: Conversation context manager
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a context manager that builds rich NPC context from world state so NPCs respond in-character.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/services/conversation/context-manager.ts
buildContext(characterId, playerId, worldId, sessionId) returns full conversation context object
Context includes character data: name, Big Five personality traits, occupation, workplace, family, romantic status, friendships/enemies, current emotional state, current location, time of day
Context includes world data: name, era, language(s), cultural setting
Context includes relationship with player: friendship level, romance stage, trust, previous conversation topics
Context includes language-learning directives: target language, player proficiency, learned vocabulary list
Context serializes to under 4000 tokens
Typecheck passes
Unit test: context builder produces correct structure from mock world data

userStories.title: gRPC server — text streaming endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a gRPC server that handles text-based conversation streams with sub-500ms first-token latency.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/services/conversation/grpc-server.ts using @grpc/grpc-js
Server listens on configurable port (default 50051), separate from Express HTTP server
Implement ConversationStream handler for text input: receives TextInput messages, streams back TextChunk responses
Text response chunks stream token-by-token from LLM provider (not buffered until complete)
Session management: create/resume/end conversation sessions with persistent context (using context-manager)
Graceful stream termination on client disconnect
Health check RPC for monitoring
Server starts alongside Express server in server/index.ts
Typecheck passes
Unit test: mock LLM provider, verify streaming text chunks arrive incrementally

userStories.title: gRPC server — session management and concurrency
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the gRPC server to handle concurrent sessions with conversation history.
acceptanceCriteria: Session store: in-memory map of session_id to conversation state (history, character context, active status)
Session creation: auto-create on first message with new session_id
Session resume: restore conversation history when same session_id reconnects
Session end: cleanup on SystemCommand.END or client disconnect
Session timeout: auto-cleanup sessions idle for >30 minutes
Concurrent session support: handle 50+ simultaneous conversation streams without blocking
Conversation history: maintain last 20 messages per session for context window
Typecheck passes
Unit test: concurrent sessions operate independently, session timeout works

userStories.title: NPC conversation memory service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NPCs to remember previous conversations so relationships feel meaningful.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/services/conversation/npc-memory.ts
Store conversation summaries per NPC-player pair in MongoDB (not full transcripts — key points only)
Summary includes: topics discussed, promises made, questions asked, emotional tone, language used
Conversation count tracking per NPC-player pair
Key fact extraction: store player-shared information (name, origin, quest details)
Memory retrieval: getMemory(npcId, playerId) returns summary for context-manager to include
Memory update: after conversation ends, auto-summarize and store (using LLM provider with short prompt)
Typecheck passes
Unit test: memory stores, retrieves, and updates correctly

userStories.title: NPC-to-NPC conversation engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NPCs to autonomously converse with each other so the world feels alive.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/services/conversation/npc-conversation-engine.ts
initiateConversation(npc1Id, npc2Id, worldId, topic?) starts NPC-NPC conversation
Topic selection driven by: shared location, relationship history, personality compatibility, recent events
Conversation uses LLM with both NPC personalities in system prompt (alternating speakers)
Conversations run 3-8 exchanges based on personality (extroverts talk longer)
NPCs speak in the world's language(s)
Conversation results update relationship values between the two NPCs
Rate limiting: max 3 concurrent NPC-NPC conversations per world
Fallback to template-based conversations (existing AmbientConversationSystem) when LLM unavailable
Emit events to GameEventBus: ambient_conversation_started, ambient_conversation_ended
Typecheck passes
Unit test: conversation respects personality weights, rate limiting works

userStories.title: STT provider interface and implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a speech-to-text provider so players can speak to NPCs via microphone.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/services/conversation/stt/stt-provider.ts with ISTTProvider interface
Interface: streamTranscription(audioStream) returns AsyncIterable<TranscriptionResult>
TranscriptionResult includes: text, isFinal, confidence, languageDetected
Create Google Cloud Speech-to-Text provider as default implementation
Streaming transcription: partial results arrive as player speaks (not waiting for silence)
Integrate into gRPC server: AudioChunk input → STT → text → LLM → response
Typecheck passes
Unit test: mock STT provider returns streaming transcription results

userStories.title: TTS provider interface and implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a text-to-speech provider so NPCs can speak their dialogue aloud.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/services/conversation/tts/tts-provider.ts with ITTSProvider interface
Interface: synthesize(text, voice, options) returns AsyncIterable<AudioChunk>
Options include: languageCode, speakingRate, pitch, encoding (opus/pcm)
Create Google Cloud TTS provider as default implementation
Sentence boundary detection: split LLM output at sentence boundaries for TTS pipelining
Voice assignment: map character attributes (gender, age, personality) to voice profiles
Minimum 8 distinct voice profiles (4 male, 4 female)
Language-correct voices: use native voices for world's language
Typecheck passes
Unit test: TTS provider returns streaming audio chunks, voice assignment maps correctly

userStories.title: gRPC server — audio streaming integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the gRPC server to handle full audio pipeline: STT input, LLM processing, TTS output.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend ConversationStream handler to accept AudioChunk input
Pipeline: AudioChunk → STT (streaming) → text → LLM (streaming) → TTS (streaming) → AudioChunk + TextChunk response
Pipeline parallelism: while LLM generates sentence N+1, TTS synthesizes sentence N
Stream TextChunk and AudioChunk responses concurrently (text arrives before audio)
First audio playback target: within 1 second of player finishing input
Connection pooling: keep gRPC channels warm between conversations
Typecheck passes
Integration test: send mock audio, receive streaming text + audio response

userStories.title: Viseme generation for lip sync
userStories.description: As a developer, I need viseme data generated from TTS output so NPC mouths move when speaking.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/services/conversation/viseme/viseme-generator.ts
Generate viseme data from text input (phoneme-to-viseme mapping)
Support Oculus OVR viseme format (15 visemes: sil, PP, FF, TH, DD, kk, CH, SS, nn, RR, aa, E, ih, oh, ou)
Viseme timing synchronized with TTS audio chunk timestamps
Stream FacialData messages in gRPC response alongside corresponding AudioChunks
Configurable quality: full viseme (15 phonemes), simplified (5 mouth shapes), disabled
Graceful fallback: return simple open/close timing when full viseme unavailable
Typecheck passes
Unit test: text input produces correctly timed viseme sequence

userStories.title: Client-side streaming audio player
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a streaming audio player in Babylon.js that plays gRPC audio chunks smoothly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/StreamingAudioPlayer.ts
Receives audio chunks, decodes via Web Audio API (AudioContext, AudioBufferSourceNode)
Pre-buffer 2-3 chunks before starting playback to prevent stuttering
Seamless chunk concatenation: no audible gaps between chunks
Spatial audio: NPC voice volume attenuates with distance from player camera
Interrupt handling: stop current audio when new conversation starts or player walks away
Audio ducking: reduce ambient/music volume during NPC speech (integrate with AudioManager)
Playback state events: onStart, onChunkPlayed, onComplete callbacks
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Client-side lip sync controller
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a lip sync controller that applies viseme data to NPC face meshes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/LipSyncController.ts
Receives FacialData from gRPC stream, applies viseme weights to character mesh morph targets
Smooth interpolation between viseme poses (not snapping)
Synchronized with StreamingAudioPlayer playback timing
Fallback: simple open/close mouth animation when no morph targets available on mesh
Start/stop lip sync on conversation begin/end
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integrate streaming conversation into BabylonChatPanel
userStories.description: As a player, I want the chat panel to use the new gRPC streaming service for real-time conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: Update BabylonChatPanel.ts to connect to gRPC-Web endpoint instead of direct Gemini API calls
Text streams token-by-token into chat panel (typewriter effect)
Audio playback begins automatically when audio chunks arrive (using StreamingAudioPlayer)
Lip sync activates on NPC mesh during audio playback (using LipSyncController)
Microphone input: push-to-talk button sends AudioChunk stream to gRPC
Fallback: if gRPC unavailable, fall back to existing direct Gemini API flow
Show visual indicators: thinking (dots), speaking (audio wave icon), listening (mic active)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Navigation mesh generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a navigation mesh generated from the world so NPCs can pathfind between locations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/NavigationSystem.ts
Generate navigation mesh from world terrain and building footprints at world load time
Use Babylon.js RecastJSPlugin (or recast-navigation WASM) for navmesh generation
NavMesh excludes building interiors (solid walls), water bodies, impassable terrain
NavMesh includes roads, paths, open ground, building doorway entry points
findPath(startPos, endPos) returns Vector3[] waypoint array
Path smoothing: remove unnecessary waypoints for natural movement
Debug visualization: toggle-able navmesh overlay (wireframe mesh)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: A* pathfinding on navigation mesh
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pathfinding that finds optimal routes and handles dynamic obstacles.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement A* pathfinding on the navigation mesh (or use RecastJS crowd/query API)
getReachableLocations(fromPos, maxDistance) returns reachable Location[] for NPC decision-making
Dynamic obstacle avoidance: NPCs steer around other NPCs and the player
Path invalidation: recalculate if obstacle appears on current path
Performance: pathfinding budget of 5 calculations per frame, queue remainder
Pathfinding for 50 NPCs completes within 16ms frame budget
Debug visualization: toggle-able path lines for selected NPC
Typecheck passes
Unit test: pathfinding finds valid routes between known positions, avoids obstacles

userStories.title: NPC movement controller
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NPCs to physically walk along pathfinding routes with natural movement.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/NPCMovementController.ts
moveTo(targetPosition, speed?, callback?) initiates movement along pathfinding route
Walking speed varies: stroll (1.5 m/s), walk (3 m/s), hurry (5 m/s)
NPCs rotate to face movement direction with smooth turning (slerp, not snapping)
Animation integration: play walk animation during movement, idle when stopped (use existing animation groups on mesh)
Arrival detection: trigger callback when NPC reaches destination (within 0.5m tolerance)
Queue system: NPCs can queue sequential movement commands
Interrupt: new moveTo cancels current movement and reroutes
NPC stops and faces player when player initiates conversation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Daily schedule execution engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the existing routine system to drive actual NPC movement through their daily schedules.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/ScheduleExecutor.ts
Reads NPC routine data (from routine-system.ts TimeBlocks) and converts to movement commands
Game time system: configurable day/night cycle (default 1 real minute = 1 game hour)
Schedule phases: sleep (22-06), morning (06-07), commute to work (07-08), work (08-17), commute home (17-18), evening (18-22)
Personality-modified schedules: high conscientiousness = early riser (05:00), low = night owl (23:00)
NPCs walk to correct location at each schedule transition via NPCMovementController
Work-shift awareness: day-shift 08-17, night-shift 20-05
Interruption handling: player conversation pauses schedule, resumes after
Schedule catch-up: if NPC is late, skip to current phase rather than replaying past phases
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Jobsite validation and auto-generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need every employed NPC to have a valid workplace building so schedules don't break.
acceptanceCriteria: Create validation in world generation: verify each NPC's currentOccupationId → occupation.businessId → business.lotId → lot has a building
Business-to-building-type mapping: Bakery → bakery, LawFirm → office, Restaurant → restaurant, Hospital → hospital, etc.
If NPC has occupation but no corresponding business exists, auto-generate business and building on available lot
If no lots available, create new lot for the auto-generated building
Validation report: log warnings for any broken NPC-occupation-business-building chain
Expose endpoint: GET /api/worlds/:worldId/validate/occupations returning chain status
Typecheck passes
Unit test: world with employed NPCs validates all chains; broken chain triggers auto-generation

userStories.title: NPC animation controller and state machine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an animation controller for NPCs with smooth state transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/NPCAnimationController.ts
Animation state machine with states: idle, walk, run, talk, listen, work, sit, eat, wave, sleep
Smooth blending between states via crossfade (0.2-0.5 second transition)
Animation speed scaling: walk animation speed matches NPC movement speed
Idle variation: random sub-animations (weight-shift, look-around) on timer so NPCs don't all idle identically
Directional facing: NPC faces conversation partner during dialogue
Animation events: callback system for animation milestones
Use existing animation groups loaded on NPC meshes; fallback to idle if specific animation missing
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Animation asset manifest and lazy loading
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pipeline for loading and managing NPC animations with lazy loading.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/AnimationAssetManager.ts
Define animation manifest: JSON mapping animation names to asset paths and metadata
Lazy loading: animations load on first use, not at world start
Animation sharing: same animation data shared across NPCs with same skeleton type
Fallback: if specific animation missing, fall back to category default (e.g., missing knead_dough → work_standing)
Minimum animation set defined: idle, walk, run, talk, listen, wave, work_standing, work_sitting, sit, eat, drink, sleep
Support Mixamo GLB animation import format
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Occupation activity system
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NPCs to perform visible work activities at their job sites.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/OccupationActivitySystem.ts

Map occupation types to activity animation sequences: Baker (work_standing), Bartender (work_standing, pour), Blacksmith (work_standing, hammer), Shopkeeper (work_standing, idle), Guard (walk patrol waypoints), Teacher (talk, gesture), Generic (work_standing)
Activity cycle: NPCs rotate between 2-3 work activities every 30-120 game-minutes
Break behavior: 1-2 breaks during work shift (sit, eat animation)
Customer interaction: shopkeeper NPCs can be interrupted by player for commerce
NPCs positioned at appropriate work locations within building bounds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Building wall collision system
userStories.description: As a player, I expect I can't walk through building walls and must use doors.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/BuildingCollisionSystem.ts
Generate simplified box collision meshes for all building walls at building creation time
Door openings: collision mesh has gaps at door positions (one door per building minimum)
Player character blocked by wall colliders (Babylon.js physics impostor or moveWithCollisions)
NPC characters also blocked by wall colliders
Collision uses simplified geometry (boxes), not full building visual mesh
Collision meshes set to invisible (not rendered, only physics)
Buildings on dedicated collision group so raycasts can distinguish building hits
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Building entry and interior loading with transitions
userStories.description: As a player, I want to enter buildings through doors with a loading-screen transition to interior scenes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/BuildingEntrySystem.ts
Door detection: when player within 2m of building door, show 'Enter [Building Name]' prompt (floating UI)
Entry trigger: player presses E key or clicks prompt
Loading transition: fade-to-black (0.5s) → load interior (using existing BuildingInteriorGenerator) → fade-from-black (0.5s)
Player spawns at interior door position facing into room
Exit: door inside interior returns player to overworld at building exterior door position
UI indicator: show current building name while inside
Pause overworld NPC movement while player is in interior (performance optimization)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Interior NPC placement and behavior
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NPCs to appear inside buildings when the player enters.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/InteriorNPCManager.ts
When interior loads, query which NPCs should be present: employees during work hours, owner during business hours, visitors per schedule
Position NPCs at role-appropriate furniture: shopkeeper behind counter, bartender behind bar, patron at table
NPCs inside perform occupation activities (from OccupationActivitySystem)
Player can initiate conversation with interior NPCs
Interior NPCs react to player entry: shopkeeper greets, bartender asks order
NPC count cap: max 6 per interior for performance (prioritize employees, then visitors by relationship)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: NPC socialization controller
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NPCs to autonomously socialize with each other based on personality.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/NPCSocializationController.ts
NPCs at the same location evaluate socialization every 5 game-minutes
Socialization probability based on: extroversion, relationship level, mood, time of day
When socializing: NPCs walk toward each other, face each other, play talk/listen animations
Trigger NPC-NPC conversation (server-side engine) when socialization starts
Post-conversation: relationship values update (friendship, trust, romance spark if applicable)
Personality-driven behavior: introverts seek quiet corners, extroverts gather in groups
If player is within overhearing range (25 units), conversation text streams to client with vocabulary highlighting
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: TotT-style dynamic decision making — action grammar hierarchy
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NPCs to make dynamic decisions via Ensemble-style action grammar rather than rigid schedules.
acceptanceCriteria: Enhance VolitionSystem.ts with action grammar hierarchy: start (high-level goals) → non-terminal (strategies) → terminal (concrete actions)
Example: be_social (start) → find_friend (non-terminal) → walk_to_tavern + greet_friend (terminal)
Each terminal action uses existing softmax personality-weighted selection
Situational modifiers: health (rest if injured), emotional state (avoid social if angry), recent events
Schedule override: high-priority volition fires override routine (e.g., angry NPC confronts rival instead of going to work)
Return-to-schedule: after volition detour, NPC resumes normal schedule
Goal persistence: unfinished goals carry to next evaluation cycle
Typecheck passes
Unit test: high-neuroticism NPC chooses different actions than low-neuroticism NPC given same situation

userStories.title: Merchant inventory auto-generation by business type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need merchant NPCs to have auto-generated inventories matching their business type.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/services/merchant-inventory.ts

Map business types to inventory categories: Bakery → food items, Blacksmith → weapons/tools/materials, Tavern → drinks/food, General Store → mixed, Apothecary → potions/herbs, Clothier → armor/fabric, Bookshop → collectibles
Auto-generate 8-15 items per merchant at world creation based on business type
Item names and descriptions generated in world's language if language-learning world
Price variation: ±20% from base, influenced by merchant personality (agreeable → lower)
Merchant gold reserve: 200-1000 based on business size
Inventory refresh: merchants restock daily (game-time)
Typecheck passes
Unit test: bakery merchant gets food items, blacksmith gets weapons/tools

userStories.title: Buy/sell transaction system
userStories.description: As a player, I want to buy and sell items with merchant NPCs using gold currency.
acceptanceCriteria: Enhance BabylonShopPanel.ts with proper transaction flow
Player gold tracked and displayed; merchant gold reserve tracked
Buy: player pays price, item added to inventory, merchant gold increases
Sell: player receives sell price (50% of base), item removed, merchant gold decreases
Sell modifier: merchant personality affects sell multiplier (agreeable pay more)
Item rarity price multipliers: common 1x, uncommon 3x, rare 10x, epic 50x, legendary 200x
Quantity support: buy/sell multiple stackable items
Gray out items player can't afford; show insufficient funds message
Merchant refuses items they don't deal in (blacksmith won't buy food)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Language-integrated purchase flow
userStories.description: As a language learner, I want purchasing items to require target-language interaction to reinforce vocabulary.
acceptanceCriteria: Enhance BabylonShopPanel.ts with language-learning mode (activated when world has target language)
NPC greets in target language when shop opens
Item names displayed in target language with native-language tooltip on hover
Beginner mode: point-and-click with vocabulary display ('You bought pain (bread)')
Intermediate mode: player types item name in target language to purchase (fuzzy match, accept minor errors)
Difficulty auto-scales based on player proficiency level from LanguageProgressTracker
Pronunciation practice: click item name to hear TTS pronunciation
Vocabulary tracking: purchased items added to player learned vocabulary list
Merchant responds in target language (Voilà!, Merci!, Vous n'avez pas assez d'or)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: NPC simulation LOD and performance optimization
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NPC simulation to scale to 50+ characters without frame drops.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/NPCSimulationLOD.ts

LOD tiers: Near (<30m) full animation/pathfinding/lip sync; Medium (30-100m) simplified walk/idle only; Far (>100m) position-only teleport to schedule location; Off-screen position update on schedule transition only
Staggered updates: spread NPC evaluations across 10-frame window (not all on same frame)
Object pooling: reuse NPC mesh instances for characters entering/leaving player area
Animation instancing: share animation evaluation across NPCs playing same animation
Frame budget monitor: if frame time exceeds 16ms, reduce NPC update frequency
Typecheck passes
Performance test: 50 NPCs in world maintain 60fps (verify no frame drops in browser dev tools)

userStories.title: World state serialization — save and load
userStories.description: As a player, I want NPC positions, inventories, and relationship changes to persist across sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/WorldStateManager.ts
Save state includes: NPC positions, current schedule phase, player inventory, player gold, player position, relationship values, romance stages, emotional states, merchant inventories
Auto-save every 5 minutes and on game exit
Save via API: POST /api/worlds/:worldId/game-state with JSON body
Load on game start: restore all state from last save
Support 3 save slots per world
Incremental saves: only transmit changed data (diff against last save)
Typecheck passes
Integration test: save state, reload, verify NPC positions and inventory match

userStories.title: Insimul JavaScript/TypeScript SDK package
userStories.description: As a web game developer, I want a JS SDK that connects to the Insimul conversation service for any web game.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/insimul-sdk-js/ as standalone npm package
InsimulClient class: connects to Insimul gRPC-Web endpoint
Methods: startConversation(characterId, options), sendText(text), sendAudio(audioChunk), endConversation()
Event callbacks: onTextChunk, onAudioChunk, onVisemeData, onActionTrigger, onError
Session management: automatic session_id tracking, resume after disconnect
Audio utilities: startMicCapture(), stopMicCapture() with Opus encoding
StreamingAudioPlayer class for chunk-based playback (Web Audio API)
API key authentication with world ID scoping
Full TypeScript type definitions
Bundle size target: <50KB minified+gzipped
README with quickstart guide
Typecheck passes
Unit test: mock gRPC connection, verify event callbacks fire

userStories.title: Insimul Unreal Engine plugin
userStories.description: As an Unreal developer, I want a plugin that connects to the Insimul conversation service.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/insimul-plugin-unreal/ with standard UE plugin structure
UInsimulSubsystem (GameInstanceSubsystem): manages gRPC connection
UInsimulChatbotComponent (ActorComponent): attach to any NPC for conversation
gRPC client using UE async task system (no main thread blocking)
UInsimulAudioCaptureComponent: wraps platform microphone APIs
UInsimulAudioStreamer: streaming TTS playback via UAudioComponent
UInsimulFaceSync: applies viseme data to morph targets on skeletal mesh
All key functions exposed as BlueprintCallable/BlueprintAssignable
Configuration: API key, server URL via editor details panel
Supported UE versions: 5.3+
README with setup guide
Compiles without errors on target UE version

userStories.title: Insimul Unity plugin
userStories.description: As a Unity developer, I want a plugin that connects to the Insimul conversation service.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/insimul-plugin-unity/ as Unity Package Manager (UPM) package
InsimulManager MonoBehaviour singleton: manages gRPC connection and session state
InsimulNPC MonoBehaviour: attach to any GameObject for conversation
gRPC client using grpc-dotnet for native builds, gRPC-Web for WebGL
InsimulMicrophone: wraps Unity Microphone class with Opus encoding
InsimulAudioPlayer: streams TTS to AudioSource component
InsimulLipSync: applies viseme weights to SkinnedMeshRenderer blend shapes
Inspector UI: custom editor for API key, server URL, character mappings
UnityEvents for onTextReceived, onConversationStarted, onConversationEnded
Supported Unity versions: 2022.3 LTS+
README with setup guide and sample scene
Compiles without errors; passes package validation

userStories.title: Insimul Godot plugin
userStories.description: As a Godot developer, I want a plugin that connects to the Insimul conversation service.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/insimul-plugin-godot/ as Godot addon (res://addons/insimul/)
InsimulClient Node: autoloaded singleton managing server connection
InsimulNPC Node: attach as child of CharacterBody3D for conversation
Transport: WebSocket-based with server-side gRPC-to-WebSocket bridge (Godot lacks native gRPC)
Audio capture: wrap AudioEffectCapture for microphone input
InsimulAudioPlayer: streams TTS to AudioStreamPlayer3D
InsimulLipSync: applies viseme data to BlendShape on MeshInstance3D
Signals: text_received, audio_chunk_received, conversation_started, conversation_ended
Export variables: API key, server URL, character ID in Inspector
GDScript API: all public methods callable from GDScript
Supported Godot versions: 4.2+
README with setup guide
Plugin passes Godot addon validation

userStories.title: gRPC-to-WebSocket bridge for Godot and browser fallback
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a WebSocket bridge so that clients without gRPC support can use the conversation service.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/services/conversation/ws-bridge.ts
WebSocket server (on configurable port, default 50052) that proxies to internal gRPC conversation service
Client sends JSON messages over WebSocket, bridge translates to gRPC ConversationRequest protobuf
Bridge translates gRPC ConversationResponse back to JSON and sends over WebSocket
Audio data sent as binary WebSocket frames (not base64 JSON)
Session management: WebSocket connection maps 1:1 to gRPC stream
Reconnection: if WebSocket drops, client can reconnect and resume session
Used by Godot plugin and as browser fallback when gRPC-Web proxy unavailable
Typecheck passes
Unit test: WebSocket message round-trips correctly through bridge

userStories.title: Export pipeline — bundle Insimul plugins into exported games
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the export pipeline to automatically include and configure Insimul plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: Babylon.js exporter: bundle @insimul/sdk into exported project, configure with world API key and character IDs
Unreal exporter: include insimul-plugin-unreal in Plugins folder, pre-configure character mappings in DefaultGame.ini
Unity exporter: include insimul-plugin-unity as UPM package, pre-configure InsimulManager
Godot exporter: include insimul-plugin-godot in addons folder, pre-configure autoload
All exporters: embed character-to-NPC mapping (game character → Insimul character ID)
All exporters: include conversation service URL configuration
Typecheck passes
Integration test: export Babylon.js project, verify SDK is included and configured

userStories.title: Response latency metrics and adaptive quality
userStories.description: As a developer, I need latency metrics across the conversation pipeline to monitor and optimize performance.
acceptanceCriteria: Add timing instrumentation to each pipeline stage: STT duration, LLM first-token, LLM total, TTS first-chunk, TTS total, total end-to-end
Log p50/p95/p99 latencies per stage (rolling window of last 100 conversations)
Expose metrics via API: GET /api/metrics/conversation returns latency percentiles
Adaptive quality: if end-to-end latency exceeds 2 seconds, automatically reduce TTS quality or skip visemes
Client-side prediction: show typing indicator immediately on send (before server responds)
Typecheck passes
Unit test: metrics collection calculates percentiles correctly
